steps.title: Translate each game into an equation
steps.content:
Each coin is worth $c$ points and each star is worth $s$ points, so total score = (coins)($c$) + (stars)($s$).

Game 1: 20 coins and 10 stars for 700 points gives $20c + 10s = 700$.

Game 2: 25 coins and 12 stars for 850 points gives $25c + 12s = 850$.

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
The correct system must match $20c + 10s = 700$ and $25c + 12s = 850$.
  • A. $10c + 20s = 700$ swaps the coin and star counts; eliminated.
  • B. $20c + 10s = 700$ and $25c + 12s = 850$ matches both games exactly.
  • C. Places 700 and 850 as coefficients of $s$, which is dimensionally wrong; eliminated.
  • D. Places 700 and 850 as coefficients of $c$ with 10 and 12 on the right side; eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
